title: Phelipanche pallens
taxonomy: Phelipanche pallens is classified as a species of parasitic flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the genus Phelipanche, which includes several species of broomrape plants. The genus Phelipanche is further classified under the family Orobanchaceae, which comprises around 200 species of parasitic plants. Orobanchaceae is a family of flowering plants that are commonly found in various parts of the world. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Phelipanche pallens and other related species.

intro: Phelipanche pallens, also known as the pale broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phelipanche pallens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Phelipanche pallens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and as a natural expectorant in traditional medicine.
